name: Ivanti ITSM Administrator
summary: Owns day-to-day configuration and support of Ivanti Neurons for ITSM, including objects, forms, workflows, notifications, searches, dashboards and operational support processes.
focusAreas: Business objects, relationships and field design
Forms, layouts, validation, permissions and workspaces
Saved searches, quick actions and automation entry points
Email templates, notifications, approvals and escalations
Dashboards, reports, queues and operational monitoring
Release testing, regression validation and support handoff
mustKnow: Explain how you structure a new service management workflow end to end.
Describe how you design a saved-search-driven notification without creating noise.
Show how you balance usability, governance and data quality in forms.
Discuss troubleshooting for workflow failures, email issues and record visibility problems.
scenarios: Automate a weekly report for incidents matching specific keywords.
Add a new service request experience with approvals and journal updates.
Enhance a form for self-service users while preserving admin controls.

name: Ivanti ITSM Developer
summary: Builds extensible configurations and custom logic on the ITSM platform, using workflows, scripting, integrations, reusable templates and controlled customization patterns.
focusAreas: Configuration versus customization decisions
Workflow automation, quick actions and business-rule-like logic
REST integrations, web service calls and JSON payload handling
Reusable templates, naming conventions and deployment safety
Performance tuning and traceable change design
Testing strategy for custom features and release promotion
mustKnow: Explain when to script and when to solve with configuration.
Describe a secure API integration approach with authentication handling.
Walk through how you debug custom logic in a lower environment before promotion.
Explain how you design upgrades and maintenance with minimal technical debt.
scenarios: Create an outbound integration that enriches incidents from a third-party tool.
Design a quick action that emails a filtered weekly ticket summary.
Build conditional form logic that respects role-based visibility.

name: Ivanti Neurons Consultant
summary: Guides platform adoption across products, aligns stakeholders on capabilities, maps requirements to product areas and turns operational pain points into automation-driven solutions.
focusAreas: Discovery workshops and requirement mapping
Capability fit across Neurons platform products
Roadmap planning and phased implementation strategy
Governance, stakeholder communication and risk management
Value articulation through automation, self-service and analytics
Change management and adoption planning
mustKnow: Explain how you evaluate product fit across multiple Ivanti capabilities.
Describe how you turn pain points into a sequenced delivery roadmap.
Show how you manage stakeholder expectations when platform limits appear.
Discuss how you measure adoption and operational outcomes after rollout.
scenarios: Recommend a first-phase rollout for an organization using multiple endpoint and service tools.
Create a roadmap balancing quick wins with future automation maturity.
Handle disagreement between service desk and security stakeholders on process design.

name: Ivanti Platform Engineer
summary: Supports architecture, deployment readiness, platform configuration standards, integrations, monitoring and cross-product alignment across the Ivanti estate.
focusAreas: Environment strategy and configuration standards
Integration governance and secure connectivity
Identity, roles, permission models and access design
Platform observability, release readiness and support models
Cross-product data flow and ownership boundaries
Operational resiliency and documentation discipline
mustKnow: Describe how you define platform guardrails across teams.
Explain operational ownership between platform, service and endpoint teams.
Discuss dependency mapping for connected tools and APIs.
Walk through a production change plan with rollback and validation.
scenarios: Set up standards for naming, security and deployment readiness.
Coordinate a cross-product release with multiple downstream dependencies.
Investigate a platform-wide access issue after a role change.

name: Ivanti Endpoint Manager Administrator
summary: Administers endpoint operations including device inventory, software distribution, patching, OS deployment and policy-based management for managed endpoints.
focusAreas: Endpoint discovery and inventory accuracy
Software packaging and software distribution methods
Patch management strategy and deployment windows
Device groups, targeting logic and rollout waves
Agent health, compliance and operational troubleshooting
Change control for endpoint-wide deployments
mustKnow: Explain how you reduce deployment risk for large software rollouts.
Describe endpoint grouping and targeting strategies.
Discuss patch exception handling and remediation follow-up.
Show how you troubleshoot devices that fail distribution or patch scans.
scenarios: Roll out a critical application to multiple device groups with phased validation.
Investigate why a subset of devices missed a patch window.
Design a compliance view for deployment success and failures.

name: Ivanti UEM Engineer
summary: Works across discovery, endpoint intelligence, self-healing, user experience and policy-based control to improve resilience and operational efficiency in modern endpoint environments.
focusAreas: Unified endpoint visibility and control models
Self-healing and bot-driven remediation patterns
Patch, compliance and endpoint experience alignment
Automation design for proactive endpoint operations
Integration with identity, security and service workflows
Operational analytics for endpoint health and user productivity
mustKnow: Explain how UEM improves operations beyond traditional endpoint administration.
Describe when to automate remediation and when to escalate to service workflows.
Discuss user experience considerations for autonomous actions.
Explain how you present endpoint health metrics to leadership.
scenarios: Design a self-heal flow for recurring endpoint performance issues.
Align endpoint event signals with ticket creation or technician intervention.
Propose a maturity model for moving from reactive to proactive endpoint operations.

name: Ivanti Neurons for MDM Engineer
summary: Designs and manages enrollment, compliance, app distribution, security and lifecycle controls for modern devices across iOS, Android, macOS, Windows and ChromeOS environments.
focusAreas: Enrollment models and ownership scenarios
Compliance policies, device posture and enforcement actions
App distribution, profiles, restrictions and security baselines
BYOD versus corporate-owned device strategy
Device troubleshooting, audit trails and exception handling
Operational reporting for device health and compliance
mustKnow: Describe common enrollment methods and their tradeoffs.
Explain how compliance policies interact with user experience and risk.
Discuss how you test app deployment before broad release.
Show how you troubleshoot a failed enrollment or unenforced restriction.
scenarios: Set up onboarding for a mixed fleet of corporate-owned and BYOD devices.
Design a staged deployment for a business-critical mobile app.
Investigate devices not receiving policies after enrollment.

name: Ivanti Velocity Specialist
summary: Optimizes mobile workflows for warehouses, field operations and supply chain environments using Velocity for modernization, browser enablement, automation, scanning and voice-related enhancements.
focusAreas: Legacy telnet and web app modernization
Workflow redesign for mobile workers
Scanning, peripherals and process efficiency
Browser-based sessions and operational automation
User experience for rugged devices and high-volume tasks
Rollout validation in business-critical environments
mustKnow: Explain what business problem Velocity solves.
Describe how you modernize host applications without rebuilding core systems.
Discuss operational risks in warehouse or field mobility rollouts.
Explain how you validate productivity and user adoption improvements.
scenarios: Convert a legacy terminal workflow into a mobile-friendly process.
Reduce picking errors using scanning and guided workflow changes.
Plan a rollout for a rugged device fleet in a warehouse environment.

name: Ivanti Automation and Integration Specialist
summary: Connects Ivanti workflows with adjacent systems using APIs, event-driven logic, automation assets and orchestration patterns that reduce manual work and improve response speed.
focusAreas: REST API design and payload handling
Authentication, secrets and secure integration patterns
Error handling, retries and idempotent workflow design
Trigger conditions, orchestration and automation boundaries
Observability for integrations and action outcomes
Documentation and supportability of automated flows
mustKnow: Describe a safe outbound integration pattern.
Explain how you handle retries without duplicating actions.
Discuss monitoring and support for failed automation runs.
Explain how you document field mappings and ownership.
scenarios: Create an integration that synchronizes ticket data with a third-party platform.
Build a low-touch automation for repetitive service desk actions.
Design an alert-to-action workflow with validation checkpoints.

name: Ivanti Solution Architect
summary: Defines end-to-end solution direction across Ivanti products, balancing business priorities, technical fit, security, implementation complexity and future scalability.
focusAreas: Capability mapping across the broader Ivanti portfolio
Reference architecture and integration design
Governance, security and data stewardship models
Roadmaps, sequencing and implementation constraints
Decision frameworks for product fit and customization
Executive communication and architecture tradeoff management
mustKnow: Explain how you decide between adjacent product capabilities.
Describe how you sequence enterprise rollout without overloading teams.
Discuss how you handle conflicting stakeholder requirements.
Explain how you create scalable architecture while limiting complexity.
scenarios: Design a multi-product architecture for service, endpoint and mobile operations.
Recommend a phased deployment strategy for a global organization.
Defend a platform decision to leadership with risk and value tradeoffs.
